info.name: WebAI Bridge API
info.description:
Complete API collection for WebAI Bridge — Auth, Gemini Connection, Chat, Agents, Conversations, Messages, Models, User Profile, Admin (Agents / Documents / Users).

Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8000

After login or register, copy the token from the response and paste it into the collection variable {{token}}.
Admin endpoints require a token belonging to a user with role='admin'.

variable.description: Paste your JWT token here after login
Replace with a real conversation UUID
Replace with a real message UUID
Replace with a real agent UUID (auto-saved by Create Agent)
Replace with a real user ID (integer)

request.description: Ping the server. No auth required. Returns ok if the service is running.
Check whether the NATS JetStream subscriber is connected. No auth required. Use this to verify the user-sync pipeline is alive.
Create a new local user account. Returns a JWT token on success. Token is auto-saved to the {{token}} variable. New local accounts always get role='user'.

Login with email and password. Tries Laravel auth first (if LARAVEL_AUTH_URL is set) then falls back to local bcrypt. Returns a JWT token valid for 7 days plus the user's role. Token is auto-saved to the {{token}} variable.
Returns the current logged-in user info including role. Call this on app load to check if the token is still valid and to know whether to show admin UI.
Manually save the user's Gemini cookies. Get these values from browser DevTools → Application → Cookies → gemini.google.com.

query.description: Options: chrome, firefox, brave, edge, safari

request.description: Auto-extract Gemini cookies from the browser on the same machine as the server. Always returns 200 — check the 'success' field in the response body.
Check if the current user has Gemini cookies saved. Use this to decide whether to show the Connect Gemini screen or the chat UI.
Remove the user's Gemini cookies and drop their active session.

Send a single message and get a streaming SSE response. Does NOT save to the database. Pass agent_id (UUID string) to activate an agent's instructions and knowledge base. When an agent_id is given the user must be assigned to that agent, otherwise 403 is returned.

Response is Server-Sent Events (text/event-stream). Each chunk:
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"Hello"}}]}

Ends with:
data: [DONE]
List all active agents the current user is assigned to. Use the returned IDs as agent_id in chat requests. Returns an empty list if the user has no assignments.

response.name: 200 Success

request.description: Get the public information for a specific agent. The user must be assigned to this agent. Does NOT return the system instructions (admin-only field).

response.name: 200 Success

request.description: List all conversations for the logged-in user. Ordered by most recently updated. Supports pagination via limit and offset.
Create a new empty conversation. Optionally pass agent_id to associate it with an agent. The returned conversation.id is auto-saved to {{conversation_id}}.
Get a single conversation and all its messages in one call. Use this when the user opens a past conversation.
Update the title or model of a conversation. Send only the fields you want to change.
Delete a single conversation and all its messages.
Delete ALL conversations for the logged-in user. Also deletes all messages inside them.
Send a message inside a conversation. Saves user message + AI response to the database automatically. Returns a streaming SSE response.
Get paginated messages for a conversation.
Delete a specific message.
Get the list of available Gemini models.
Get the full profile of the logged-in user including preferences and role.
Update user preferences.
Explicit logout. After calling this, clear the token from localStorage on the frontend.
Get Gemini connection status with available models.
Disconnect Gemini. Same as DELETE /api/cookies.

item.name: Admin — Agents
item.description: All endpoints in this folder require a token with role='admin'. Returns 403 for regular users.

request.description: List all agents (active and inactive). Admin only.

response.name: 200 Success

request.description: Create a new AI agent with custom instructions. The agent_id is auto-saved to {{agent_id}}. model is optional (defaults to gemini-3-flash). instructions becomes the system prompt injected at chat time.

response.name: 200 Success

request.description: Get full agent details including instructions. Admin only.

response.name: 200 Success

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Support Bot v2",
  "description": "Improved support agent",
  "model": "gemini-3-pro",
  "instructions": "You are an advanced support agent. Be concise and factual.",
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: Update any field of an agent. Send only the fields you want to change. Set is_active=false to deactivate without deleting.
Soft-delete (deactivate) an agent. Sets is_active=false. The agent and its documents are preserved in the database but it will no longer be available to users.

item.name: Admin — Documents
item.description: Knowledge base document management. Documents are chunked, embedded with Gemini text-embedding-004, and stored in pgvector. Top-5 chunks are injected as context at chat time.

request.description: Upload a knowledge document for the specified agent. The file is split into ~1500-char chunks with 150-char overlap, embedded via Gemini text-embedding-004 (768 dims), and stored in pgvector. Supported: PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD. Use multipart/form-data with key 'file'.

response.name: 400 Unsupported Format

request.description: List all documents uploaded for an agent, with filename and chunk count per file.
Delete all chunks for a specific document from the agent's knowledge base. Replace 'product-manual.pdf' in the URL with the actual filename. Use URL-encoding for filenames with spaces.

item.name: Admin — User Assignments
item.description: Manage which users have access to which agents. A user can only use an agent in chat if they are assigned to it.

request.description: List all users currently assigned to this agent.
Assign one or more users to an agent. user_ids is an array of integer user IDs. Already-assigned users are silently ignored (idempotent).
Remove a single user from an agent. Replace {{user_id}} with the integer user ID.
List all users in the system with their role, external_id (Laravel user ID if synced via NATS), and synced_at timestamp.
Promote or demote a user. role must be 'admin' or 'user'. Replace {{user_id}} with the integer user ID. An admin cannot change their own role via this endpoint.
Debug only — checks if the user's Gemini session exists in the WebAI engine memory.
